title: Evaluate build-time firmware timestamp as GNSS/time epoch seed
description: User suggested using build-time stamps in firmware or board metadata to epoch GNSS/time. Evaluate software-only design: how build epoch is embedded, trust/freshness limits, interaction with GNSS cold start and network/manual time sources, and what hardware validation remains for RTC/GNSS behavior. Create implementation follow-ups if accepted.
notes: User clarified the build-time timestamp idea: use build-time stamps in firmware or board metadata to epoch GNSS/time. Evaluation should compare immutable firmware build epoch, board/provisioning metadata epoch, and trust/staleness interactions with GNSS cold start, RTC, network/manual time, and rollback risk.
close_reason: Completed as design confirmation and de-duplication: existing closed Beads project-LICHEN-xj8k, project-LICHEN-ecq7, project-LICHEN-2067, and project-LICHEN-2067.1 already accept and implement the no-hardware policy that firmware build epoch and optional authenticated board/provision epoch are lower-bound freshness floors for GNSS/time, not authoritative clocks. Confirmed current implementation surfaces in docs/firmware-time-provider.md, lichen/cmake/lichen_build_epoch.cmake, lichen/subsys/lichen/hal, lichen/tests/build_epoch, lichen/tests/hal, and lichen/tests/puck_location. Physical validation remains blocked/tracked by existing GNSS hardware Beads. Created follow-ups project-LICHEN-7rmw, project-LICHEN-vfdi, and project-LICHEN-okar for newly identified spec/status/provision-persistence gaps.
